measurementNote: Project-switch timings include Computer Use action and accessibility-tree refresh overhead; they are conservative upper-bound observations, not low-level event timings.

terminalLoad.details: A packaged shell rendered seq 1 4000. Two additional shells then emitted 2000 delayed lines each with overlapping execution.

multiPane.details: The active project reached four panes for the recorded performance sample and five panes after Gemini launch.

geminiTui.details: Gemini started in a packaged pane and rendered trust/auth UI. A separate non-persistent prompt smoke failed with HTTP 400 API_KEY_INVALID. No credential was stored or copied into evidence.

oneProjectEditAgentPreview.details: Structured Codex changed exactly one requested heading in exactly one file. The packaged editor rendered the changed source, a blank Shell pane served it on port 4173, and the embedded Browser rendered the changed heading.

twoAgentSameProject.details: Structured Codex chats preserve independent provider runs. Optional raw terminals also passed packaged isolation: pre-relaunch PIDs 59144/62755, post-relaunch PIDs 84110/89143, independent labels/output, and switch-back without restart.

controlledComparison.details: A controlled Keelhouse workload was captured. A separate extension-free VS Code process launched, but macOS accessibility targeting selected the user's existing VS Code windows, so equivalent integrated-terminal state was not driven or certified.

stateRestore.details: The isolated test profile was restored byte-for-byte; before and after checksums matched.
